hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 electrostatic interactions, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with strong SSTR2 binding but insufficient selectivity. The next iteration will focus on disrupting conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to increase Δmargin and achieve true SSTR2 selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions known to mediate off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5). Focusing mutagenesis on these positions will help push Δmargin positive.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved pocket interactions in off-target SSTRs while maintaining diversity and avoiding loss of SSTR2 binding.
